description: Define the core Pydantic models and TypedDicts that structure the state for the LangGraph Parent and Child graphs to ensure strict schema validation across the application.

userStories.title: Define Core Entity Schemas
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want to define strict Pydantic models for the core data entities (JobTrackerEntry, BaseResumeSchema, JobDetailsSchema) so that the application safely validates and parses inputs without data corruption.
acceptanceCriteria: Given the templates.MD specifications, When I instantiate the models with valid data dictionaries, Then the models parse successfully without validation errors.
Given the models are defined, When I instantiate them with missing required fields, Then a ValidationError is raised.

userStories.title: Define Workflow AI Schemas
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want to define Pydantic models for the AI reasoning outputs (ClarificationQuestion, ResumeDiffSchema) so that the HITL nodes receive predictable, structured data from the LLM.
acceptanceCriteria: Given the templates.MD JSON diff schema, When the ResumeDiffSchema is instantiated, Then it enforces the exact action/section/old_value/new_value/reason format.
Given the ClarificationQuestion schema, When instantiated, Then it enforces the presence of an ID, type, question text, and options array.

tasks.description: Define ClarificationQuestion Pydantic model in src/schemas.py
Define ResumeDiffSchema Pydantic model in src/schemas.py

userStories.title: Define LangGraph State TypedDicts
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want to define the LangGraph State TypedDicts (ParentGraphState and ChildGraphState) so that data flows safely between graph nodes with strict type checking.
acceptanceCriteria: Given the defined Pydantic models, When I define ParentGraphState and ChildGraphState, Then the types correctly reference the Pydantic models.
Given the defined TypedDicts, When running a static type checker (mypy), Then the file passes without errors.
